qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Denial of service via Slowloris attack due to missing HTTP server timeouts in parse-server
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause and code path: this finding concerns missing HTTP server timeout configuration in src/ParseServer.ts, while the advisory concerns PostgreSQL adapter SQL injection from unescaped dot-notation Increment sub-key names.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause: SQL injection in Parse Server's PostgreSQL storage adapter when a nested object field name used in an Increment operation is interpolated into SQL string literals without escaping single quotes.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-gqpp-xgvh-9h7h describes Parse Server PostgreSQL adapter SQL injection via dot-notation sub-key names in Increment operations.
slowloris_dos_no_http_timeout reports missing HTTP timeout configuration in src/ParseServer.ts and denial of service through slow partial HTTP requests.

The manifest-recognized qualifying report does not reference the PostgreSQL storage adapter, nested Increment operation handling, dot-notation sub-key escaping, or an equivalent SQL source-to-sink path.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding was present, so PoC execution was not applicable under the judgement instructions.
